summarization.a: The figure shows the thickness of WO₃ after exposure to O₂/O₃ and BCl₃/HF as a function of the number of half-cycles.
summarization.b: The figure illustrates the thickness of W as a function of the number of half-cycles, with an etch rate of 2.44 Å/cycle.

data_extraction.a:
| Number of Half-Cycles | WO₃ Thickness (Å) After O₂/O₃ | WO₃ Thickness (Å) After BCl₃/HF |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 35 | 27 |
| 10 | 42 | 36 |
| 20 | 37 | 30 |
| 30 | 32 | 25 |
| 40 | 29 | 22 |
| 50 | 26 | 21 |
| 60 | 25 | 17 |

a.question: Is the etch process constant over time?
a.answer: a	It seems that in the initial stages of the process, there still is an increase in thickness of WO3, but it seems to settle towards a constant thickness after 50 half cycles

a.question: Does the shown trend necessarily mean that the etching of W is also not constant over time?
a.answer: a	No, it does not. It could be that the initial thickness of W enables the modification step to have a larger effect, whilst the etching of W remains relatively constant.

b.question: What are the HF, BCl3 and O2/O3 exposures?
How can the etch rate be determined from this graph?
b.answer: b	By determining the slope and multiplying this with 2, as the x-axis denotes half-cycles and not full cycles.
